THE FINAL WEIGHT OF THE FRAME, $ W_f $, CAN BE EXPRESSED AS $ W_f = W \times (1 - 0.02)^n $. THIS EQUATION ACCOUNTS FOR THE 2% WEIGHT REDUCTION PER ADVANCED COMPONENT ADDED.

TO ACHIEVE A TOTAL REDUCTION OF ENERGY EXPENDITURE BY 25%, THE FUNCTION $ E(n) = 1 - (1 - 0.02)^n $ MUST EQUAL 0.25. SOLVING FOR $ n $, WE GET $ 0.25 = 1 - (0.98)^n $, WHICH SIMPLIFIES TO $ (0.98)^n = 0.75 $. TAKING THE NATURAL LOGARITHM OF BOTH SIDES GIVES $ n \times \ln(0.98) = \ln(0.75) $, LEADING TO $ n = \ln(0.75) / \ln(0.98) $. CALCULATING THIS, $ n $ IS APPROXIMATELY 13.51. SINCE $ n $ MUST BE AN INTEGER, THE MINIMUM NUMBER OF ADVANCED COMPONENTS REQUIRED IS 14.

THE CLOCK TICKS ON AS TIME IS OF THE ESSENCE FOR ALEX TO PREPARE. THE CLOCK CONTINUES TO MOVE FORWARD AS THE GOAL IS CLEAR. THE CLOCK REMINDS US THAT PRECISION AND TIMING ARE CRITICAL IN THIS DECISION.
